claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Child Benefit is assessed for each individual child, so let me start with:

**Do you have a child (or children) you're responsible for? If yes, how old is the child (or how old are each of the children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I have a child I'm responsible for. My son Alex is 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. So you have a son, Alex, who is 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is 15 (under 16), he automatically qualifies as a qualifying child in terms of age. Now I need to check a few more things:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's just at school like other kids his age.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to ask about benefits:

**Is Alex receiving Income Support or certain other benefits in his own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just 15, so he doesn't get anything like that himself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, that's clear. Now let me ask:

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship? (If yes, what type and where - England, Scotland, or Wales?)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship. He's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to check about your residency status:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I need to check about your responsibility for Alex:

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (for example, another parent or guardian)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Now I need to understand Alex's living situation:

**Is Alex currently:**
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is being fostered by me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex is being fostered by you, I need to understand the fostering arrangement:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance as part of the fostering arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't get anything from the council for fostering Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why Alex qualifies:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He is not working 24 hours or more per week
- He is not receiving benefits in his own right
- He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- You normally live in the UK
- You live with Alex and are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance

**Next steps to claim:**
1. Apply online or by post using form CH2
2. Provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
3. Provide Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate

**Important note:** Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

Payments are typically made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great news, thank you so much for explaining everything! I understand what I need to do now to claim. I appreciate your help.
